 {"id":"bd-2ky9","title":"E2E Test Scripts with Comprehensive Logging","description":"# E2E Test Scripts with Comprehensive Logging

## Background
Plan Section Testing + Verification requires end-to-end tests that validate the complete pipeline from user input to terminal output. The user specifically requested "comprehensive unit tests and e2e test scripts with great, detailed logging."

## E2E Test Philosophy
E2E tests should:
1. Test the complete system as a user would experience it
2. Run in a PTY to capture real terminal behavior
3. Log every step for post-mortem debugging
4. Be deterministic and reproducible
5. Cover happy paths and error cases

## E2E Test Script Structure

### Directory Layout
```
tests/
  e2e/
    fixtures/          # Test input files
    expected/          # Expected output snapshots
    scripts/           # Test runner scripts
      run_all.sh       # Master test runner
      test_inline.sh   # Inline mode tests
      test_altscreen.sh # Alt-screen tests
      test_cleanup.sh  # Cleanup/panic tests
      test_input.sh    # Input handling tests
    lib/
      common.sh        # Shared utilities
      logging.sh       # Logging functions
      pty.sh           # PTY spawning helpers
```

### Logging Framework (logging.sh)
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Comprehensive logging for E2E tests

LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-DEBUG}
LOG_FILE=${LOG_FILE:-/tmp/ftui_e2e_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log}

log() {
    local level=$1
    shift
    local timestamp=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N")
    local caller="${BASH_SOURCE[2]}:${BASH_LINENO[1]}"
    echo "[$timestamp] [$level] [$caller] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_debug() { [[ "$LOG_LEVEL" == "DEBUG" ]] && log "DEBUG" "$@"; }
log_info()  { log "INFO" "$@"; }
log_warn()  { log "WARN" "$@"; }
log_error() { log "ERROR" "$@"; }

log_test_start() {
    local test_name=$1
    log_info "=========================================="
    log_info "STARTING TEST: $test_name"
    log_info "=========================================="
}

log_test_pass() {
    local test_name=$1
    log_info "✓ PASSED: $test_name"
}

log_test_fail() {
    local test_name=$1
    local reason=$2
    log_error "✗ FAILED: $test_name"
    log_error "  Reason: $reason"
    log_error "  Log file: $LOG_FILE"
}

log_hex_dump() {
    local label=$1
    local data=$2
    log_debug "$label (hex): $(echo -n "$data" | xxd -p | tr -d n)"
}
```

## E2E Test Categories

### 1. Inline Mode Tests (test_inline.sh)
Tests that streaming logs + UI chrome works correctly.

```bash
test_inline_log_scroll() {
    log_test_start "Inline mode: log scrolling"
    
    # Setup
    local pty_output=$(mktemp)
    log_debug "PTY output file: $pty_output"
    
    # Run ftui app that streams 100 log lines with 6-line UI
    log_info "Spawning ftui inline mode app..."
    timeout 10s script -q -c "./target/debug/ftui-harness --mode inline --ui-height 6 --log-count 100" "$pty_output"
    local exit_code=$?
    log_debug "Exit code: $exit_code"
    
    # Verify
    log_info "Verifying output..."
    
    # Check scrollback preservation
    if grep -q "Log line 1" "$pty_output"; then
        log_debug "Early log lines found in scrollback ✓"
    else
        log_test_fail "Inline log scroll" "Early log lines missing from scrollback"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check UI region intact
    if tail -6 "$pty_output" | grep -q "\[Status\]"; then
        log_debug "UI region present at bottom ✓"
    else
        log_test_fail "Inline log scroll" "UI region not at expected position"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_test_pass "Inline mode: log scrolling"
    rm "$pty_output"
}
```

### 2. Cleanup Tests (test_cleanup.sh)
Tests that terminal is restored after exit/panic.

```bash
test_panic_cleanup() {
    log_test_start "Panic cleanup: terminal restored"
    
    # Capture terminal state before
    local before_state=$(stty -g)
    log_debug "Terminal state before: $before_state"
    
    # Run app that panics
    log_info "Running app that will panic..."
    ./target/debug/ftui-panic-test 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Capture terminal state after
    local after_state=$(stty -g)
    log_debug "Terminal state after: $after_state"
    
    # Verify restoration
    if [[ "$before_state" == "$after_state" ]]; then
        log_debug "Terminal state restored ✓"
    else
        log_test_fail "Panic cleanup" "Terminal state not restored"
        log_error "  Before: $before_state"
        log_error "  After:  $after_state"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check cursor visible (SGR sequence check)
    log_info "Checking cursor visibility..."
    # ... additional checks ...
    
    log_test_pass "Panic cleanup: terminal restored"
}
```

### 3. Input Tests (test_input.sh)
Tests for keyboard, mouse, paste handling.

### 4. ANSI Output Tests
Verify presenter output matches expected sequences.

## Test Output Format

Each test run produces:
1. Console output (PASS/FAIL per test)
2. Detailed log file with timestamps
3. JSON summary for CI
4. PTY captures for failed tests

### JSON Summary Format
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-31T23:45:00Z",
  "total": 50,
  "passed": 48,
  "failed": 2,
  "skipped": 0,
  "duration_ms": 12345,
  "tests": [
    {
      "name": "test_inline_log_scroll",
      "status": "passed",
      "duration_ms": 234,
      "log_lines": 45
    },
    {
      "name": "test_panic_cleanup",
      "status": "failed",
      "duration_ms": 567,
      "error": "Terminal state not restored",
      "log_file": "/tmp/ftui_e2e_20260131_234500.log"
    }
  ]
}
```

## E2E Test Inventory

| Test | Description | Priority |
|------|-------------|----------|
| inline_basic | Basic inline mode display | P0 |
| inline_log_scroll | Log scrolling preserves scrollback | P0 |
| inline_resize | Resize re-anchors UI | P0 |
| inline_cursor | Cursor position contract | P0 |
| altscreen_enter_exit | Alt-screen lifecycle | P0 |
| cleanup_normal | Terminal restored on exit | P0 |
| cleanup_panic | Terminal restored on panic | P0 |
| cleanup_io_error | Terminal restored on IO error | P1 |
| input_key_basic | Basic key parsing | P0 |
| input_key_modifiers | Modifier key detection | P1 |
| input_mouse_click | Mouse click handling | P1 |
| input_mouse_scroll | Mouse scroll handling | P1 |
| input_paste | Bracketed paste handling | P1 |
| input_focus | Focus events | P2 |
| ansi_sgr | SGR sequence output | P0 |
| ansi_osc8 | OSC 8 hyperlink output | P1 |
| ansi_sync | Synchronized output | P1 |
| unicode_basic | ASCII width handling | P0 |
| unicode_wide | CJK wide chars | P0 |
| unicode_emoji | Emoji rendering | P0 |
| unicode_zwj | ZWJ sequences | P1 |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] run_all.sh executes all E2E tests
- [ ] Each test has detailed logging
- [ ] Failed tests produce actionable diagnostics
- [ ] JSON summary generated for CI
- [ ] PTY captures preserved on failure
- [ ] Tests run in < 60 seconds total
- [ ] All P0 tests must pass for v1

## Dependencies
- Requires: bd-2kj (Agent Harness exists to test)
- Requires: bd-10i.11.2 (PTY framework)
- Blocks: bd-2gx (Quality Gates)

## CI Integration
- Run on every PR
- Block merge on P0 test failures
- Upload log artifacts on failure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:22:54.577032739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:49:32.441537371Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:49:32.441432504Z","close_reason":"Implemented E2E scripts + harness env hooks; full run passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","depends_on_id":"bd-2kj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":373,"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"E2E suite: 31 tests, 6 suites, all passing in 39s.  {"id":"bd-3j8t","title":"Implement Virtualized<T> container with visible range calculation","description":"# Implement Virtualized<T> container with visible range calculation

## Background
Part of Progressive Rendering (bd-3t87). The core container that tracks scroll position and calculates which items are visible.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Virtualized content container
pub struct Virtualized<T> {
    /// All items (owned or reference to external storage)
    items: VirtualizedStorage<T>,
    
    /// Current scroll offset (in items)
    scroll_offset: usize,
    
    /// Number of visible items (computed from area height)
    visible_count: usize,
    
    /// Overscan: extra items rendered above/below visible
    overscan: usize,
    
    /// Height per item
    item_height: ItemHeight,
    
    /// Whether to auto-scroll on new items
    follow_mode: bool,
    
    /// Scroll velocity for momentum
    scroll_velocity: f32,
}

/// Storage strategy for items
pub enum VirtualizedStorage<T> {
    /// Owned vector of items
    Owned(Vec<T>),
    
    /// External storage with fetch function
    External {
        len: usize,
        fetch: Box<dyn Fn(Range<usize>) -> Vec<T>>,
    },
}

/// Height calculation strategy
pub enum ItemHeight {
    /// All items have fixed height
    Fixed(u16),
    
    /// Items have variable height, cached
    Variable(HeightCache),
}

/// LRU cache for measured item heights
pub struct HeightCache {
    cache: LruCache<usize, u16>,
    default_height: u16,
}

impl<T> Virtualized<T> {
    /// Create with owned storage
    pub fn new(items: Vec<T>) -> Self {
        Self {
            items: VirtualizedStorage::Owned(items),
            scroll_offset: 0,
            visible_count: 0,
            overscan: 2,
            item_height: ItemHeight::Fixed(1),
            follow_mode: false,
            scroll_velocity: 0.0,
        }
    }
    
    /// Create with external storage
    pub fn external<F>(len: usize, fetch: F) -> Self
    where
        F: Fn(Range<usize>) -> Vec<T> + 'static,
    {
        Self {
            items: VirtualizedStorage::External { len, fetch: Box::new(fetch) },
            scroll_offset: 0,
            visible_count: 0,
            overscan: 2,
            item_height: ItemHeight::Fixed(1),
            follow_mode: false,
            scroll_velocity: 0.0,
        }
    }
    
    /// Total number of items
    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
        match &self.items {
            VirtualizedStorage::Owned(v) => v.len(),
            VirtualizedStorage::External { len, .. } => *len,
        }
    }
    
    /// Calculate visible range given viewport height
    pub fn visible_range(&self, viewport_height: u16) -> Range<usize> {
        let items_per_screen = match &self.item_height {
            ItemHeight::Fixed(h) => (viewport_height / h) as usize,
            ItemHeight::Variable(cache) => {
                // Sum heights until we exceed viewport
                let mut count = 0;
                let mut total_height = 0u16;
                let start = self.scroll_offset;
                while total_height < viewport_height && start + count < self.len() {
                    total_height += cache.get(start + count);
                    count += 1;
                }
                count
            }
        };
        
        self.visible_count = items_per_screen;
        
        let start = self.scroll_offset;
        let end = (start + items_per_screen).min(self.len());
        start..end
    }
    
    /// Get range with overscan for smooth scrolling
    pub fn render_range(&self, viewport_height: u16) -> Range<usize> {
        let visible = self.visible_range(viewport_height);
        let start = visible.start.saturating_sub(self.overscan);
        let end = (visible.end + self.overscan).min(self.len());
        start..end
    }
    
    /// Get item by index
    pub fn get(&self, idx: usize) -> Option<&T> {
        match &self.items {
            VirtualizedStorage::Owned(v) => v.get(idx),
            VirtualizedStorage::External { .. } => None, // Must use fetch
        }
    }
    
    /// Update external storage length
    pub fn set_len(&mut self, len: usize) {
        if let VirtualizedStorage::External { len: ref mut l, .. } = &mut self.items {
            *l = len;
        }
    }
}

impl HeightCache {
    pub fn new(default_height: u16, capacity: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            cache: LruCache::new(NonZeroUsize::new(capacity).unwrap()),
            default_height,
        }
    }
    
    pub fn get(&self, idx: usize) -> u16 {
        *self.cache.get(&idx).unwrap_or(&self.default_height)
    }
    
    pub fn set(&mut self, idx: usize, height: u16) {
        self.cache.put(idx, height);
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Virtualized<T> with owned and external storage
- [ ] visible_range() calculates correctly for fixed height
- [ ] visible_range() calculates correctly for variable height

 {"id":"bd-3k3x","title":"Demo Showcase: Performance HUD + Render Budget Visualizer","description":"On-screen HUD showing frame time, diff size, and degradation/budget state.

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Demo Showcase: Performance HUD + Render Budget Visualizer
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests
- Epic includes a perf budget + regression suite (hyperfine + flamegraphs + CI gates) with explainable failures


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.
- Drift/Regimes: if runtime metrics vary (resize storms, frame pacing), incorporate change-point detection and regime-specific policies.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.
- Add JSONL perf logs with schema: run_id, case, env, features, seed, metrics, budgets, pass/fail, and artifact paths.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:36.007195298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:25:18.057496522Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:25:18.057415820Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","performance","ux"],"comments":[{"id":429,"issue_id":"bd-3k3x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background
- FrankenTUI already tracks budget and diff sizes. A HUD makes performance tuning visible.

Goals
- Display frame time, diff cell count, render budget state.
- Toggle between minimal and detailed views.
- Optional logging of HUD metrics.

Testing
- Unit tests for metric collection and formatting.
- PTY E2E tests verifying HUD toggle and output logs.

 {"id":"bd-3k3x.1","title":"Performance HUD spec + UX flow","description":"Define HUD metrics, layout, and toggles.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Performance HUD spec + UX flow
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
